outbound_official.notes: Source is Benin's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MAEC) official page listing bilateral visa-suppression/exemption agreements: diplomatie.gouv.bj/fr/representations/visas (reciprocal mutual exemptions) and diplomatie.gouv.bj/fr/representations/visas/african_country (African-country exemptions). The reciprocal tab states 'NB: Les ressortissants de ces pays peuvent également entrer et circuler librement au Bénin aux mêmes conditions indiquées' — confirming these are mutual and Beninese citizens enjoy the same access to those countries. The African tab lists countries where both sets of nationals may enter under identical conditions. ECOWAS free movement (15 member states) is grounded in Protocol A/SP.1/5/79 (Dakar, 25 May 1979). The linked PDF (www.gouv.bj/article/850, dated September 2020) is the government's dedicated outbound exemption list but is mostly image-based; the live web pages on diplomatie.gouv.bj are the up-to-date source used here, showing agreements through June 2025. Only entries where ordinary passport (passeport national/ordinaire) is explicitly listed are included in visa_free; diplomatic/service-only agreements are excluded from this list.

visa_free.notes: All nationals of ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States) member states are exempt from visa requirement under Protocol A/SP.1/5/79 of Dakar dated 25 May 1979 on the free movement of persons, the right of residence and establishment. DEI services page explicitly states e-visa is issued to nationals of 'foreign countries outside ECOWAS or without a visa exemption agreement with Benin'. ECOWAS members: Benin, Burkina Faso, Cabo Verde, Cote d'Ivoire, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia, Mali, Mauritania, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Togo.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not exempt (non-ECOWAS, no bilateral agreement covering ordinary passport)
e_visa.notes: E-visa available online via evisa.bj before arrival — visa on arrival is NOT available. Options: 30-day single entry (50 EUR self-apply / 60 EUR via embassy), 30-day multiple entry (75 EUR / 85 EUR), 90-day multiple entry (100 EUR / 110 EUR). Processing approx. 24 hours.

visa_policy.notes: Benin's visa policy has three tiers: (1) ECOWAS nationals enter visa-free with no formalities under the 1979 Dakar Protocol; (2) nationals of countries with bilateral visa waiver agreements enter visa-free — most agreements apply to diplomatic/service passports only, but several newer agreements (Belize, Dominica, Grenada, Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Antigua and Barbuda, Bahamas, Barbados, Malaysia, Philippines, Saint Vincent, Mauritius, São Tomé and Príncipe, Seychelles, Kenya, South Africa, Tunisia, Rwanda, Central African Republic, Chad, Congo-Brazzaville, Burundi) explicitly include ordinary passports; (3) all other foreign nationals must obtain an e-visa online at evisa.bj BEFORE arrival — no visa on arrival. The official bilateral agreements list is maintained at diplomatie.gouv.bj/fr/representations/visas (reciprocal exemptions tab) and diplomatie.gouv.bj/fr/representations/visas/african_country (African countries unilateral exemptions tab). The African tab is labeled 'PAYS QUI SONT EXEMPTES DE VISA D'ENTRÉE AU BÉNIN SANS EXIGENCE DE RÉCIPROCITÉ' (countries exempt without reciprocity requirement).

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program exists in Benin. There is a diaspora nationality recognition scheme — 'Recognition of Beninese Nationality for Afro-descendants' under Law No. 2024-31 — operated via myafroorigins.bj. Eligibility: persons of sub-Saharan African ancestry deported in the transatlantic slave trade (NOT open to general investors; sub-Saharan African state nationals explicitly excluded). Fee: $100 USD. Processing: 3 months. This is an ancestry/diaspora recognition program, NOT a CBI scheme.

programs.name: Carte Zone Économique Spéciale (ZES) — Special Economic Zone Resident Card
programs.notes: Issued exclusively to foreign investors and operators working in the Glo-Djigbé Industrial Zone (GDIZ), located 45 km from Cotonou. Valid for 5 years. Card fee: 350,000 XOF. No minimum investment amount stated on the official DEI page. Application via service-public.bj online.

programs.name: Carte de résident ordinaire (Ordinary Resident Card)
programs.notes: Available to any foreigner who can justify means of subsistence or a guarantee of support and has good moral standing. Valid 3 years. Card fee: 50,000 XOF for ECOWAS nationals; 250,000 XOF for non-ECOWAS nationals. Renewable; after two renewals may qualify for Privileged Resident status.

programs.name: Carte de résident privilégié (Privileged Resident Card)
programs.notes: Available after holding and renewing ordinary resident card twice, plus demonstrating permanent employment, immovable property ownership, conjugal residence, or a functioning business/commercial/industrial establishment in Benin. Valid 10 years. Card fee: 100,000 XOF for ECOWAS nationals; 800,000 XOF for non-ECOWAS nationals.

agreements: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States) — free movement of persons, right of residence and establishment under Protocol A/SP.1/5/79 of Dakar, 25 May 1979
UEMOA (West African Economic and Monetary Union) — free movement under Article 91 of the UEMOA Treaty

REMOVED XOF pricing from e-visa notes: XOF prices were included in the prior record (32,798/49,197/65,596 XOF) but were NOT confirmed by the evisa.bj costs article fetched in this session (article only listed EUR prices). Retained EUR pricing which is confirmed.
REMOVED reference to 'Loi N° 2025-15 du 02 Juillet 2025 and Décret N° 2026-049 du 04 mars 2026' from e-visa notes: these were not verifiable from the official page in this session.

CONFIRMED: No professional/business/study e-visa types were added to visa_policy — these are domestic DEI service categories, not nationality-based grants; they do not alter the basic nationality grant classification.
CONFIRMED: Singapore ordinary passport = 14 days, dipl/service = 30 days — as in prior record.
CONFIRMED: ECOWAS free movement remains in visa_free with null max_stay_days.

CONFIRMED: All reciprocal tab entries (Belize, Dominica, Grenada, Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Antigua, UAE, Bahamas, Barbados, Malaysia, Philippines, Saint Vincent, Sri Lanka, and pre-existing entries) match official MFA page scraped with Firecrawl.
CONFIRMED: cbi has_program=false — no CBI program found.
CONFIRMED: rbi programs unchanged — DEI page confirms same three residence card types and fees.
gaps: ECOWAS free movement maximum stay duration not stated verbatim on the official page — exemption implied by DEI services text excluding ECOWAS nationals from e-visa requirement.
The ZES investor residence card has no published minimum investment amount on the official DEI page — only the card fee (350,000 XOF) is specified.
No official digital-nomad, startup, talent, or fast-track skilled migration visa scheme found on official Benin government websites.
Barbados, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Malaysia, Philippines: no agreement date is listed on the official MFA page for these countries.
Namibia: no agreement date listed on official MFA page.
Macao SAR: no agreement date listed on official MFA page.

The african_country tab is labeled 'PAYS QUI SONT EXEMPTES DE VISA D'ENTRÉE AU BÉNIN SANS EXIGENCE DE RÉCIPROCITÉ' (countries exempt WITHOUT reciprocity requirement) — meaning Benin unilaterally grants these exemptions; whether Benin citizens enjoy reciprocal access is not guaranteed.

No official Benin government source was found confirming any credential-based conditional entry rule (e.g. visa-free or VOA for holders of a valid US/Schengen/UK/UAE visa or residence permit). Some third-party aggregators assert such a rule but cite no official Benin source — excluded per official-source-only requirement.

XOF pricing for e-visas: the evisa.bj costs article returned only EUR prices in this session — XOF equivalents previously recorded (32,798/49,197/65,596 XOF) could not be re-confirmed and were removed.
Specific legislative reference (Loi N° 2025-15 and Décret N° 2026-049) could not be re-confirmed from official sources in this session and was removed from e-visa notes.
The 'african_country' tab may not be exhaustive — additional African country agreements may exist that have not yet been added to the official page.
